name: Aditya Parikh
headline: PhD Researcher (Responsible AI) at DTU Compute
bio: I am a doctoral researcher working on responsible AI for medical imaging and clinical vision-language models, with a focus on robustness, bias/fairness, saliency behavior, decoding effects, and clinically faithful deployment.
currentWorkSummary: Current work centers on responsible medical AI: evaluating and improving clinical reliability of vision and vision-language models under real-world bias and distribution shift.
currentWorkItems: PhD research at DTU Compute on trustworthy vision and vision-language models for radiology.
Analyzing decoding behavior, saliency alignment, and clinical terminology fidelity in report generation.
Studying fairness across age, gender, and ethnicity with robust subgroup evaluation protocols.
researchFocusAreas: Responsible AI for clinical imaging and radiology workflows
Bias, fairness, and subgroup reliability in medical machine learning
Vision-language models and report generation fidelity

workExperience.summary: Research in responsible AI for medical data, including clinical reliability and fairness of vision and vision-language models.
Built VLM and weak/single-positive learning pipelines for medical imaging to improve performance while reducing annotation effort.
Designed organ-specific encoder-decoder segmentation frameworks for laparoscopic imaging under class imbalance.
Developed adaptive filtering methods for non-stationary acoustic echo cancellation with improved convergence and efficiency.

highlightedWorks.title: Clinical Terminology Erasure in Radiology VLMs
highlightedWorks.description: Shows how conventional generation metrics can miss clinically meaningful terminology drops.

highlightedWorks.title: Label Bias and Age-Disparity Analysis
highlightedWorks.description: Visualizes subgroup disparity sources and representational shifts in segmentation performance.

highlightedWorks.title: Fair Lung Disease Diagnosis with Adversarial Attention
highlightedWorks.description: Highlights concept-level balancing strategies for chest CT classification and fairness.
